meaning: n.(集合词)暴徒,乌合之众,(盗贼等的)一群v.成群暴动,聚众滋扰
examples: An angry mob gathered outside the courthouse.
She pushed her way through the mob and confronted him face to face.
If they continue like this there is a danger of the mob taking over.
Requests to stage more mobs flooded in, says Staci Lawrence, co-founder of Flash Mob America.
Randall uses her ex-husband, who hails from a mob-involved family, to help out occasionally.
